privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.introduction: This Privacy Policy explains what personal data JigSwap collects, why we collect it, and what your rights are. JigSwap is run by Sander Verkuil, who is the controller for the processing described here. We aim to collect as little as possible and to be transparent about the rest.

informationWeCollect.title: Information We Collect
informationWeCollect.description: We collect information you give us directly, plus a limited amount of information collected automatically while you use JigSwap.

personalInformation.title: Information you provide
items: Account details via Clerk, our sign-in provider: name, email address, username, and avatar
Your profile: display name, bio, and other details you choose to share
Your puzzle library: the copies you own, their condition, photos you upload, completions, and collections
Exchange activity: swap, sale, and loan requests, the messages you send to arrange them, and reviews
Community activity: the circles you belong to, members you follow, and comments you post
Messages you send through the contact form: your name, email address, and message

automaticallyCollected.title: Collected automatically
items: Product analytics events, such as pages visited and features used (via PostHog, only after you accept the cookie notice)
Error reports and operational logs used to keep the service running
Your language preference and cookie-notice choice, stored in cookies
A push notification subscription — only if you turn push notifications on

howWeUseInformation.title: How We Use Your Information
howWeUseInformation.description: We use your data to run JigSwap — nothing more:
purposes: Provide your library, completions, collections, and insights
Facilitate exchanges between members, including messaging and loan tracking
Show your profile and puzzles to the members you have chosen to share them with
Send you notifications about activity that concerns you
Keep the service secure and prevent abuse, including automated screening of uploaded photos
Understand how JigSwap is used so we can improve it

lawfulBasis.title: Our lawful basis, in plain terms
lawfulBasis.description: Under the GDPR we need a legal reason for each use of your data. Ours are:
items: Performing our agreement with you: everything needed to run the service you signed up for — your account, library, exchanges, and messages
Legitimate interests: keeping JigSwap secure, preventing abuse, screening uploaded photos, operational logging, and understanding usage so we can improve the service
Consent: where we ask you first, such as browser push notifications
Legal obligations: the rare cases where we must keep or share data because the law requires it

dataStorage.title: Where Your Data Lives
dataStorage.description: JigSwap does not run its own servers. Your data is stored in the European Union with a small number of service providers:

convex.title: Convex
convex.description: Our database and file storage, on Convex's EU hosting. Your library, exchanges, messages, and uploaded photos are stored in the European Union.

clerk.title: Clerk
clerk.description: Our authentication provider, with EU data residency. Clerk stores your sign-in credentials and core account details (name, email address, avatar) and handles signing in. When you delete your account, Clerk notifies us and we remove your account record.

vercel.title: Vercel
vercel.description: Our hosting provider. The JigSwap web application is served through Vercel's global edge network. Vercel delivers the site to your browser; your personal data itself lives in the EU-hosted services above.

photos.title: Photos You Upload
photos.description: Photos you add to your library go through an automatic pipeline before other members can see them:
steps: The image is re-encoded, which strips hidden metadata such as EXIF data — including any embedded location
An automated content-safety check screens the image; for this we currently use a model hosted by Hugging Face, which processes the image for this purpose only
Until this finishes, the photo is visible only to you

analytics.title: Analytics and Error Reporting
analytics.description: We use analytics to understand how JigSwap is used and to catch errors before they spoil the fun.

posthog.title: PostHog
posthog.description: We use PostHog, hosted in the European Union, for product analytics and error reporting. Analytics requests are routed through our own domain. PostHog only starts after you accept the cookie notice — if you decline, it is never loaded. This data is used only to improve JigSwap.

analytics.noAdvertising: We do not show ads, we do not use your data for advertising, and we never sell your data.

dataSharing.title: Data Sharing
dataSharing.description: We share personal data only with the providers that run JigSwap, and only what they need to do their job.
dataSharing.noSale: We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal data. Ever.
dataSharing.limitedSharing: Data is shared only in these cases:
circumstances: With the providers named in this policy, as processors on our behalf: Convex (database and files), Clerk (sign-in), Vercel (hosting), PostHog (analytics and error reporting), Axiom (operational logs, EU-hosted), and Hugging Face (automated photo screening)
With other members, to the extent you choose — your profile and the parts of your library you share are visible according to your settings
When the law requires it, or to protect the service and its members against fraud or abuse
For anything else: only with your explicit consent

dataSecurity.title: Data Security
dataSecurity.description: We take reasonable measures to protect your data:
measures: All traffic between your browser and JigSwap is encrypted (TLS)
Every read and write is checked on the server: members only see what the visibility rules allow
Uploaded photos are stripped of hidden metadata and screened before they become visible to others
Access to production data is limited to what is needed to run the service

yourRights.title: Your Rights
yourRights.description: Under the GDPR you have the following rights, and JigSwap has built-in tools for most of them:
rights: Access: see the data we hold about you — most of it is directly visible in the app
Portability: download your data as a file via the export on your Insights page
Rectification: correct your profile and library yourself at any time, or ask us to fix something
Erasure: delete your account, which removes your account record — and ask us to remove any remaining content tied to it
Objection and restriction: object to processing we base on our legitimate interests
Complaint: lodge a complaint with your data protection authority — in the Netherlands, the Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.description: JigSwap uses a small number of cookies and similar technologies:
types: Sign-in cookies from Clerk that keep you logged in (strictly necessary)
A language cookie that remembers your English/Dutch preference
A cookie that remembers that you have seen the cookie notice
Analytics identifiers from PostHog, stored in cookies and local storage — only after you accept the cookie notice
cookies.control: Analytics is opt-in: it only runs after you accept the cookie notice, and declining means it is never loaded. You can also block or delete cookies in your browser settings; JigSwap keeps working, though you will need to sign in again and your preferences may reset.

cookieConsent.title: We use cookies
cookieConsent.description: We use cookies to ensure you get the best experience on our website. For more information on how we use cookies, please see our cookie policy.
cookieConsent.acceptanceText: By clicking "Accept", you agree to our use of cookies.

children.title: Children
children.description: JigSwap is not intended for children under 16, and we do not knowingly collect personal data from them. If you believe a child under 16 has an account, please contact us.

international.title: International Data Transfers
international.description: Your personal data is stored in the European Union: we use the EU-hosted variants of Convex, Clerk, PostHog, and Axiom. Our hosting provider Vercel serves the website through a global edge network, so requests may pass through servers outside the EU on their way to you, but the personal data itself stays stored in the EU. Where a provider processes limited data outside the EU/EEA, transfers take place under GDPR safeguards such as the EU standard contractual clauses.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.description: We may update this policy as JigSwap evolves. Material changes are announced by posting the new policy on this page and updating the date at the top.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Questions about privacy or your data? Email us at

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.introduction: These Terms of Service ('Terms') govern your use of JigSwap, a community platform for jigsaw puzzle enthusiasts, run by Sander Verkuil. JigSwap is a hobby project, not a commercial marketplace. By creating an account or using JigSwap, you agree to these Terms.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.description: By accessing or using JigSwap you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree with any part of them, please do not use the service.

serviceDescription.title: What JigSwap Is
serviceDescription.description: JigSwap helps jigsaw puzzle collectors keep track of their puzzles and exchange them with other members. The service includes:
features: A community-maintained puzzle catalog that members can add puzzles to
A personal library to track the copies you own, their condition, and photos of them
Logging completions (when you finished a puzzle and how long it took) and organising copies into collections
Exchanges between members — swaps, sales, and loans — with messaging to arrange them
Circles (private groups of friends) for sharing part of your library, plus member profiles and follows
Personal insights and statistics about your puzzling, including a download of your own data

userAccounts.title: Your Account
userAccounts.description: You need an account for most features. Sign-in and account management are handled by Clerk, our authentication provider. You are responsible for:
responsibilities: Keeping access to your account secure
Providing accurate information in your profile and about your puzzles
Letting us know if you suspect unauthorised use of your account
Being at least 16 years old to create an account

userConduct.title: Community Conduct
userConduct.description: JigSwap runs on trust between puzzle lovers. Use the service responsibly:
expected: Describe your puzzles honestly, including condition and missing pieces
Follow through on exchanges you agree to
Report problems or suspicious behaviour via the contact form
prohibited: Breaking the law or infringing other people's rights
Harassing, threatening, or deceiving other members
Uploading unlawful, infringing, or sexually explicit content
Spam or unsolicited commercial use of the platform
Scraping the service or attempting to gain unauthorised access to accounts or systems

trading.title: Swaps, Sales, and Loans
trading.description: JigSwap lets members arrange swaps, sales, and loans of puzzles with each other. Every exchange is an agreement between the two members involved — not with JigSwap. When you take part in an exchange, you agree to:
agreements: Accurately describe the condition and completeness of your puzzles
Honour the terms you agreed on, including returning borrowed puzzles
Arrange payment and shipping between yourselves — JigSwap does not process payments
Communicate promptly and try to resolve disagreements respectfully
trading.disclaimer: JigSwap only brings members into contact and keeps a record of the exchange. We are not a party to any exchange and are not responsible for payment, shipping, or the quality, condition, or completeness of exchanged puzzles. Exchanges are at your own risk.

intellectualProperty.title: Content and Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.description: Different things on JigSwap belong to different people:

yourContent.title: Your content
yourContent.description: Photos, reviews, comments, and other content you post remain yours. By posting, you give us a non-exclusive licence to store and display that content so the service can work — for example, showing your puzzle photos to the members you have shared them with. Puzzles you add to the shared catalog become part of the community catalog that all members can use. Uploaded photos pass through an automated safety check before other members can see them (see the Privacy Policy for details).

platform.title: The platform and puzzle artwork
platform.description: The JigSwap software is open source under the MIT licence. Puzzle images and artwork in the catalog belong to their respective publishers and rights holders and are used to identify puzzles. If you hold rights to content shown on JigSwap and want it corrected or removed, please contact us.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: How we handle your personal data is described in our Privacy Policy, which applies alongside these Terms.

termination.title: Ending Your Account
termination.description: You can stop using JigSwap and delete your account at any time. We may suspend or remove accounts that break these Terms — for example for scams, harassment, or unlawful content. Where reasonably possible, we will tell you why.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.description: JigSwap is a hobby project provided free of charge and 'as is'. We do our best to keep it running well, but we cannot promise that the service will always be available or error-free.

limitation.title: Limitation of Liability
limitation.description: To the extent permitted by applicable law, JigSwap and its operator are not liable for indirect or consequential damage arising from your use of the service or from exchanges between members. Nothing in these Terms limits liability that cannot be limited or excluded under applicable law.

changes.title: Changes to These Terms
changes.description: We may update these Terms from time to time. Material changes are announced by posting the new Terms on this page and updating the date at the top. If you keep using JigSwap after a change, the updated Terms apply.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Questions about these Terms? Email us at

about.title: About JigSwap
about.subtitle: The complete platform for jigsaw puzzle enthusiasts
about.introduction: JigSwap is more than just a puzzle trading platform. We're building a community where puzzle enthusiasts can connect, share, and grow their passion for jigsaw puzzles in a sustainable way.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: We believe that puzzles should have multiple lives. Our mission is to create a sustainable ecosystem where puzzle enthusiasts can share their completed puzzles with others, discover new challenges, and build meaningful connections within the community.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: We envision a world where every puzzle enthusiast has access to an endless variety of puzzles without the environmental impact of constant new purchases. A world where the joy of puzzling is shared and celebrated across borders and cultures.

sustainability.title: Sustainability
sustainability.description: We're committed to reducing waste in the puzzle industry by promoting reuse and sharing of completed puzzles.

community.title: Community
community.description: We believe in the power of community and fostering connections between puzzle enthusiasts worldwide.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: We operate with complete transparency in our data practices and platform operations.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.description: We strive to make puzzle trading accessible to everyone, regardless of location or economic background.

story.title: Our Story
story.description: JigSwap was born from a simple family need: we wanted to easily see what puzzles our friends and family owned so we could exchange them. Completed puzzles often sit on shelves, taking up space while others search for new challenges. We saw an opportunity to create a platform that not only solves this problem but also builds a community around the shared love of puzzling.
story.founded: Founded in 2025 as a family side project, JigSwap has grown from a simple idea to a thriving community of puzzle enthusiasts. Our platform combines modern technology with the timeless joy of jigsaw puzzles, creating an experience that's both nostalgic and innovative.

team.title: The Team
team.description: JigSwap is built by a family of puzzle enthusiasts, for puzzle enthusiasts. We started this as a side project to solve our own need for sharing puzzles with friends and family.
team.passion: We're passionate about creating a platform that serves the puzzle community while promoting sustainable practices in the hobby we all love.

technology.title: Technology
technology.description: Built with modern web technologies, JigSwap provides a seamless experience for managing collections, tracking completions, and facilitating trades.

community.title: Join Our Community
community.description: Whether you're a casual puzzler or a dedicated enthusiast, JigSwap has something for you. Join thousands of users who are already trading, connecting, and growing their puzzle collections.
community.getStarted: Ready to start your puzzle journey? Create an account and begin exploring the world of sustainable puzzle trading.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: We'd love to hear from you
contact.introduction: Have questions, suggestions, or just want to say hello? We're here to help and would love to hear from you. Our team is committed to providing excellent support to the JigSwap community.

waysToContact.title: Ways to Contact Us
waysToContact.description: Choose the method that works best for you. We typically respond within 24 hours during business days.

email.title: Email Support
email.description: For general inquiries, support questions, or feedback:

support.title: Technical Support
support.description: For technical issues, bug reports, or feature requests:
support.responseTime: We aim to respond within 12 hours for urgent issues

privacy.title: Privacy & Legal
privacy.description: For privacy concerns or legal matters:
privacy.responseTime: We respond to all privacy inquiries within 48 hours

business.title: Business Inquiries
business.description: For partnerships, collaborations, or business opportunities:
business.responseTime: We'll get back to you within 2-3 business days

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Before reaching out, you might find answers to common questions in our FAQ section.
topics: How to get started with JigSwap

feedback.title: Feedback & Suggestions
feedback.description: We're always looking to improve JigSwap. Your feedback helps us make the platform better for everyone.

response.title: What to Expect
response.description: When you contact us, here's what you can expect:
expectations: Acknowledgment of your message within 24 hours
Follow-up if additional information is needed
Escalation to appropriate team members when necessary

callToAction.title: Ready to Transform Your Puzzle Experience?
callToAction.description: Join thousands of puzzle enthusiasts who are already managing their collections, trading puzzles, and connecting with the community on JigSwap

notifications.pushServiceUnreachable: Your browser couldn't reach its push service. Some privacy browsers block or remove it — Brave disables it by default (enable 'Use Google services for push messaging' in Settings), and de-Googled Chromium builds like Helium strip it out. Firefox, Chrome, or Edge will work.

footer.tagline: Swap, lend and share jigsaw puzzles — accessible and sustainable, for the whole community.
footer.madeBy: Made with 🧩 by a family of puzzlers.

home.heroTitle: Share jigsaw puzzles with fellow enthusiasts
home.heroLead: Build your own puzzle library, lend or swap puzzles, and always see who has your lent-out boxes. Connect with puzzlers across the country.

how.title: Up and running in three steps
how.lead: No fuss, no cost to begin. That's how simple shared puzzling is.
how.step1Body: Add your puzzles with brand, piece count and condition — ready in a few taps.
how.step2Body: Browse the community, filter by difficulty and piece count, and request a puzzle to borrow or swap.
how.step3Body: Lend out for a while or indefinitely, and always see who has your puzzle.

rows.libraryTitle: A shelf that feels like home
rows.libraryBody: Your collection as real 3D boxes on a wooden plank. Track completions, condition and notes — as tactile as your real bookshelf.
rows.lendTitle: Lend out — and always know who has your puzzles
rows.lendBody: Someone wants your puzzle? Lend it out for a while or indefinitely. JigSwap keeps track of who has which box — so lending is as carefree as puzzling.
rows.lendPoint1: Lend for a while or forever
rows.lendPoint3: Ask for it back whenever you like
rows.discoverBody: Filter by piece count, difficulty, condition and category. Discover what fellow puzzlers have on their shelves and request a swap or a loan.
rows.discoverPoint3: Lend, swap or share in your circle

sustain.title: One puzzle, many hands
sustain.body: A finished puzzle doesn't need to gather dust in the attic. By sharing, lending and swapping, the community gives puzzles a second, third and fourth life — and everyone gets more joy from them.

quote.text: “We built JigSwap because our finished puzzles deserved better than the attic — and because puzzling is more fun when you share it.”
quote.name: The family behind JigSwap
quote.sub: Puzzling at the kitchen table since 2025

cta.title: Ready to share your first puzzle?
cta.body: It's free to begin. Build your shelf and meet the community.

how.heroTitle: From shelf to sharing, in five steps
how.heroLead: JigSwap makes sharing puzzles as relaxing as puzzling itself. Here's how it works, end to end.
how.step1Body: Sign up for free, pick a username and tell the community a little about yourself and the puzzles you love.
how.step2Body: Add your puzzles with brand, piece count, difficulty and condition — and an EAN if you have it handy.
how.step3Body: Browse the community, filter for what you want and request a puzzle to borrow or swap. See instantly who offers something you'd like to try.
how.step4Body: Propose a loan or a swap, agree the details together, and pick a spot to hand the puzzle over in person. Everything stays tidy in one place.
how.step5Body: Always see who has your puzzle. Ask for it back whenever you like, log your completion and leave an honest review.
how.modesTitle: Lend, swap or share in circles
how.modesLead: You decide how to share a puzzle. Every way is equally welcome.
how.mode1Body: Someone wants your puzzle? Lend it out for as long as you like — even forever. JigSwap tracks who has it, and you ask for it back whenever suits you.
how.mode2Body: Trade one finished puzzle for another. Two puzzlers, two new challenges.
how.mode3Body: Create a private circle for family or friends and share your shelf with exactly the people you choose — with per-circle permissions.
how.faq1A: Creating an account, filling your shelf, lending and swapping is free. You hand puzzles over in person, so there are no shipping costs.
how.faq2Q: Can I borrow a puzzle indefinitely?
how.faq2A: Yes. Agree with the owner how long you keep it — that can be indefinitely. The owner always sees who has the puzzle and can ask for it back.
how.faq3Q: How does the owner keep track of their puzzle?
how.faq3A: Every puzzle appears in your 'My puzzles' overview with its status and who has it: available, lent out or recalled. A recall automatically sends the borrower a notification.
how.faq4Q: How do I hand the puzzle over?
how.faq4A: In person. You agree a place and time to hand the puzzle over — easy and free of shipping costs. For now we don't arrange shipping.

features.heroLead: From a personal puzzle library to lending-with-tracking and a community you trust — here's what JigSwap gives you.
features.g1i1Body: Every puzzle with brand, piece count, condition and notes — one tidy, tactile overview.
features.g1i2Body: Group by brand, theme or mood — private or public, with your own name, colour and icon.
features.g1i3Body: Track dates, solve times and ratings — and set goals for your puzzle year.
features.g2Title: Always know who has your puzzles
features.g2i1Body: Everything you've lent out in one place — who has it and since when.
features.g2i2Body: Available, lent out or recalled — see where every box stands at a glance.
features.g2i3Title: For as long as you like
features.g2i3Body: Lend out for a while or forever, and ask for it back whenever suits you.
features.g3i1Body: Filter by piece count, difficulty, condition and category.
features.g3i2Body: See the newest puzzles in the catalog as the community adds them.
features.g3i3Body: Propose, accept and complete swaps — with clear statuses every step of the way.
features.g4i1Body: Share your shelf with family and friend groups — you decide who may see and exchange.
features.g4i2Body: Rate exchanges afterwards — honest reviews and trust levels keep swapping pleasant.
features.g4i3Body: Follow fellow puzzlers, see their activity and share your public profile.

aboutPage.heroLead: JigSwap grew out of a family side project: we just wanted an easy way to see and swap puzzles with friends and family. From there, a platform for the whole puzzle community was born.
aboutPage.missionTitle: Making puzzle sharing accessible and sustainable — for everyone
aboutPage.missionBody: We believe a finished puzzle isn't the end, but a new beginning for someone else. JigSwap connects puzzlers so every box gets opened more often.
aboutPage.value1Body: Puzzling is relaxation. We're about sharing and joy, not profit.
aboutPage.value2Body: One puzzle you lend out and that passes through many hands is worth more than ten gathering dust.
aboutPage.value3Body: We build for the people who love puzzling as much as we do.
aboutPage.value4Body: Honest reviews and transparent tracking keep every exchange safe.
aboutPage.tl1Body: Started as a simple way to swap puzzles with friends and family.
aboutPage.tl2Body: The tactile puzzle plank was born — a digital shelf that feels like home.
aboutPage.tl3Body: Puzzlers now share, lend and swap across the country.

contactPage.heroTitle: Got a question or something to share?
contactPage.heroLead: We'd love to hear from you — whether you need help, have an idea, or just want to say hi.
contactPage.emailSub: We usually reply within 1 business day.
contactPage.supportSub: Questions about a swap or your account.
contactPage.privacySub: For privacy concerns or legal matters.
contactPage.basedBody: JigSwap is an online platform — we work from home, in between puzzles.
contactPage.errorMessage: Tell us a bit more (min. 10 chars)
contactPage.errorSubmit: Something went wrong while sending — please try again.
contactPage.privacyNote: By sending, you agree to our privacy policy.
contactPage.sentBody: Thanks for reaching out. We'll get back to you as soon as we can.

legal.privacyLead: Your trust is everything. Here's how we handle your data carefully and transparently.
legal.termsLead: The ground rules that keep swapping fair and pleasant for everyone.

docs.landingLead: Everything you need to build your library, log solves, and swap puzzles with the community.
